Hi, welcome everybody to Minimum Vibe Launch 2.0. So 2.0 usually infers there was a 1.0 and we're gonna talk about that in just a second and what the difference is. But first, how did you get here, right? So you're likely in this new program, in this new mini workshop because you are already a member of TenX launches, TenX sales pages, part two, automated intimacy, six figure emails, some combination of these programs and that's going to come into play. There is a little bit of buildup, right? Of stuff that we would have learned there that comes into play in what we're doing here. If for whatever reason you somehow stumbled into this as a standalone and you don't have access to all the templates inside those other programs, use your discernment, it's no problem. You can certainly reach out to myself or Phil to see what we could do to get you access to those other courses and most of the strategies you will be learning in Minimum Vibe Launch 2.0 will function as stand-alones. You don't necessarily need to rely on everything else you're doing in those other programs. You're just going to be able to get a much fuller experience. But for now I'm going to make the assumption that you do have access to these other programs that you have learned some of the core concepts already within them and that you are ready to really build on top of it. So this course is both supplemental to what you've already learned and also to some degree standalone if you're just looking to find some new strategies to layer into the launches you're already doing. With that said, if you have had access to Minimum Vibe Launch 1.0, what is the difference? So on the left here you see this 12-day sequence that we used as MVL 1.0 in the original and many folks had amazing success with this. There's nothing broken, nothing wrong about it, but like all things that were created a few years ago, you kind of got to give it a little bit of a facelift, give it a bit of an update to match how markets have shifted, how audiences are consuming and converting right now. And we're recording this in late 2021, almost 2022. Using strategies we've really been testing in the field for the last year in our own launches and for clients. So you're going to get a bit of exposure to that as well. But essentially the first sequence that we taught was 14 emails spread out over 12 days covering all those phases of the launch we teach from the segmentation to the pre-launch to the launch day to the sale sequence to the close. And it's great, it's just a lot, right? And attention spans haven't necessarily increased since this original iteration of it. And it kind of begs the question, is 12 days too long to maintain attention and focus? And of course that question depends on the market you're in and the observations you've had in your own audience. But this time around we're going to aim for something a little bit more compact, a little bit shorter and therefore de-risk some of that drop off that we'd have if we spread it out too close to two weeks. Next thing is consumption isn't linear, right? We can't rely on arguments being built out across those 12 days, right? 12 or 14 days with this weird false assumption that marketers have that first they'll do this then they'll get this belief and then they'll be exposed to that like, no, that's not how people consume on the internet, it's probably never how people consumed in any medium ever where there's this certainty that they consume everything in perfect order. So that is one assumption we are going to be breaking away from and instead creating little pockets within the launch sequence where we can have more personalized dynamic exchanges that have that intimacy, that have that personalized aspect where we could have this conversion coaching aspect where we're interacting with them and giving them precisely what they need in that time, in that moment specific to them in order to be able to convert. Next point is trust and consent must be earned initially granted throughout and reinforced not constantly but at least at a few checkpoints within your launch. So a lot of people, a lot of audiences suffer from a bit of launch fatigue, launch exhaustion, launch annoyance if you will. And that's not to say we shouldn't be launching, not at all, but we should be getting by in early and we should be getting it often enough where we can essentially empower our audiences to participate in the launch sequence, in the launch event, in the launch, in the whole, I don't wanna say hypeness but in the whole event of the launch as they deem fit. So that's what we are going to be doing and focusing on as well. And then finally it begs the question because people love talking in superlatives and our launches dead. Of course they're not dead but I would beg to say that dead launches are dead and that doesn't make much sense but what I mean by dead launches are essentially a stack of emails that you just kind of write in advance and plug into your system and let it just kind of run without any kind of oversight, personalized intervention within it or opportunities to engage on an individual level with those who can really benefit from that conversion coaching to have the confidence to move forward. So dead launches are dead and they still will work to some degree when you have the blunt force numbers to just push through your funnel, right? When you put 30,000 person lists through a sequence, of course you're going to get sales even if it is a quote unquote dead launch but nowhere close to the level of sales you can generate if you just make your funnel, your minimum viable funnel come alive with these personalized interactions and the automations that allow it to be more scalable. So those are the things we are going to be focusing on. This is why it is time for an update to this sequence and the main goal of MVL 2.0 is really to create more personalized micro exchanges where a quick investment of personal attention or conversion coaching can tip the scales towards an empowered sales. So we are essentially creating these conversion contexts through our email sequence and you're going to see all about how that plays out. So how is this structured? In the first lesson, we're just going to go very quickly over four concepts for 2022 and beyond. Some of these may be familiar or familiar-ish if you've taken other trainings with me but we're going to do a quick recap there. Then we're just going to jump straight into the five core strategies. These can be plugin strategies. It's not something that you have to do all at once. You could use what works. You could layer them in into what you're already doing. These are very much supplemental, I would say. But the first one is going to be the exclusively empowered early access trigger. That's something you're doing right at the beginning of your launch. Then we have the mid-cart temperature check. We have the slice of life closing template which is a new closing template we're adding to the mix. And then some extras. Four and five, I would say, are a little bit more advanced but have really moved the needle when we've tested them. So there's the minority report reach out which relies on a bit of advanced automation strategies and lead scoring, which we'll talk about when we get there. And then the personalized proposal which is essentially a G-Doc, Google Doc invitation version of your long form sales page that you could still write based off of 10X sales pages but deliver in a more personalized, intimate way when the launch dynamics in your audience size warrants it. So that is how this is going to be structured. If I were to give you a structure of what that looks like top to bottom, it would be across seven days, right? Day one will be kind of your pre-launch one where you're gonna be reshifting some beliefs. Sorry, you can rely on sacred cows if you've heard that term before in our templates. And you're also going to be getting consent through binary choice of, yeah, I wanna participate in this launch, nope, please mute me out. So that's gonna be covered in strategy one. If they click on the yes, I'm into this launch link that will trigger an early access email with an expiring code. So that is Day X. It's essentially triggered upon to click of PL one. Then we're gonna do it again, option to do it again. You can always call your own audibles but I like doing this twice. So there will be a second one tackling another big sacred cow of that industry, of that market, getting consent around the launch and giving them early access. So that is essentially happening on day one and day two, which would be before the open cart is even really announced to your list. Then we'll go into the launch phase, official open cart, a sales email, a second sales email on day five. Also on day five, mid cart, mid cart temp check. The temperature check is a new one we're adding in here. You'll learn all about the nuances and application of that in strategy two. And then in those final 48 hours, while you continue to send your closing emails based on what you've already learned in 10X launches, you will be supplementing that with what we call Minority Report Reachout, which is strategy four. And then your close, your final close will be the slice of life close, which we'll touch upon in strategy three. And then option, if you don't want to design a full sales page for this, if you are doing a beta launch, if you are doing something more intimate on Facebook or via direct message, I do suggest leaning on strategy five and making this more of a Google Doc personalized proposal than a sales page which downgrades the intimacy they have with you. So that is what we are gonna be doing. And note on the templates in the workbook pack, which you should see somewhere around this video, you'll see essentially 39 email templates that are spread across 10X launches, six figure emails and seven figure emails that you can use for these various parts of your launch where you see sales one or close two or pre-launch one. So you have no shortage of templates to lean on. It is my wish however, that you learn what I call the source code of essentially what are you coaching during these various phases of the launch, what are the tools at your disposal, and then essentially empowering yourself to go more off script, right? And writing emails that are in service to those principles but are just uniquely your own and not very template-y. That said, these templates are always available for you in a pinch, they've been tested widely, and my goal is that you simply write. Whether you write better when you have free reign based on principles you know, or leaning on a template. Other way is cool, the important part is that you write well, that you write with sound conversion principles in mind, and that you layer in these strategies we're talking about here which help you go from a dead launch to an alive dynamic one with the personalized touch points within them that really offer your audiences what they need to feel confident as we move into greater levels of intimacy within our launches. So that's what I have for you in this brief introduction. Take a little bit of a breather and I'll meet you in the next lesson where we're gonna go over the four core concepts that we'll be using throughout the rest of the program. I'll see you in a bit.
